failing_first.observable: 1 failed, 2 passed; expected failure was KeyError: 'assigned_agent_name' in test_my_listings_flags_missing_knowledge_and_logistics_for_attention.

implementation_summary: Added lean index-level fields to /api/v1/listings/mine: last_activity_at, assigned_agent_name, knowledge_status, missing_fact_count, active_viewing_count, open_offer_count, buyer_conversation_count, logistics_status, primary_next_action.
Computed counts/statuses from existing listing stats, conversations, offer records, viewings, knowledge summaries/facts, listing logistics, and agent/member profile names.
Kept the response lean: no full documents, facts, offers, or logistics payloads are returned.
Kept non-managing agent scoping unchanged: seller_id or assigned_agent_id within the resolved brokerage.

size_review.frontend/src/lib/queries.ts: 608 pure LOC; pre-existing large shared API type/query file, only AgentListingSummary fields were extended within task scope
size_review.app/main.py: 281 pure LOC; pre-existing app assembly file, route mount was inspected for T3 but not edited by this remediation worker

cleanup_receipt: No long-running servers or background processes were started. Test-created DB rows use UUID-suffixed identifiers and fixture cleanup.
risks: The inventory implementation uses per-listing aggregate queries; acceptable for T3 lean contract coverage but may need batching if listing counts grow.
Open offers are approximated as non-superseded offer records because there is no separate offer lifecycle status on DBOfferRecord.
Logistics status is conservative: ready listings without confirmed logistics are needs_attention, off-plan listings are not_required.
